summary: Implement M4 withdrawal and postwar result handoff to M3 governance
acceptance_criteria: Campaign withdrawal can be ordered or triggered by explicit supply, season, siege, loss, or objective-complete reasons and updates force/supply/campaign state deterministically.
Withdrawal records distinguish orderly withdrawal, forced retreat, cancellation before departure, and failed extraction without implementing tactical pursuit or naval evacuation systems.
M4 produces an explicit WarOutcome/PostwarCandidate DTO that can be consumed by accepted M3 postwar governance preview/application paths without bypassing M3 validation.
War losses and campaign results flow back to existing economic, loyalty, obligation, and credit/reliability hooks with auditable reason codes and conservation checks.
The task does not implement full diplomacy, peace treaties, rebellion systems, formal historical content, campaign planning UI, or M5 vertical-slice content.
required_tests: Scenario tests cover planned withdrawal, rainy-season cancellation, supply-forced retreat, siege-lift withdrawal, and WarOutcome handoff into M3 governance preview.
Property/invariant tests cover no duplicate postwar candidate creation, loss accounting conservation, deterministic withdrawal ordering, and valid M3 governance references.
node -e JSON parse/shape check for M4-WITHDRAWAL-POSTWAR-HANDOFF-001

simulation_engineer.responsibility: primary M4 withdrawal and postwar result handoff writer

systems_architect.responsibility: architecture reviewer for M4 withdrawal and postwar handoff

notes: The handoff target is the accepted M3 governance prototype; do not invent a parallel postwar settlement model.
War losses must show human and political cost; do not present forced displacement, starvation, or slaughter as free rewards.

Explicit exclusions for M4 remain: manual node battle, complex naval warfare, full diplomacy, 3D battle, multiplayer/server, telemetry, arbitrary-code mods, and frozen product or architecture changes.
Promoted to READY after M4-AUTO-ENGAGEMENT-SIEGE-001 entered origin/main at b09cf6b7982f3644256a9422f824b2f2639bf000 and was CLOSED.
